title: Melted Peppery Russian Milk Dumplings
description: Inspired by traditional Russian dough pockets, these dumplings are infused with a peppery kick and a rich, molten center, featuring semi-skimmed milk as the base ingredient. A diabetic-friendly twist on a classic treat.
instructions: Combine flour, milk, eggs, sugar, thyme, black pepper, and salt in a mixing bowl to form a smooth dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until elastic and smooth.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of semi-skimmed milk in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press edges together to seal.
Cook the dumplings in a large skillet over medium heat for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Transfer the dumplings to a baking dish and bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 10-12 minutes, until the centers are molten and bubbly.
Serve the dumplings hot with a dollop of whipped cream or a sprinkle of powdered sugar.
